# stripe_subscription_item (Resource)

Subscription items allow you to create customer subscriptions with more than
one plan, making it easy to represent complex billing relationships.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `subscription` (String) The `subscription` this `subscription_item` belongs to.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `billing_thresholds` (Attributes) Define thresholds at which an invoice will be sent, and the related subscription advanced to a new billing period (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--billing_thresholds))
- `discounts` (Attributes List) The discounts applied to the subscription item. Subscription item discounts are applied before subscription discounts. Use `expand[]=discounts` to expand each discount. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--discounts))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format.
- `payment_behavior` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Controls how Stripe handles payment when a subscription update requires payment and `collection_method=charge_automatically`.
- `plan` (String) You can now model subscriptions more flexibly using the [Prices API](https://api.stripe.com#prices). It replaces the Plans API and is backwards compatible to simplify your migration.

Plans define the base price, currency, and billing cycle for recurring purchases of products.
[Products](https://api.stripe.com#products) help you track inventory or provisioning, and plans help you track pricing. Different physical goods or levels of service should be represented by products, and pricing options should be represented by plans. This approach lets you change prices without having to change your provisioning scheme.

For example, you might have a single "gold" product that has plans for $10/month, $100/year, €9/month, and €90/year.

Related guides: [Set up a subscription](https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/set-up-subscription) and more about [products and prices](https://docs.stripe.com/products-prices/overview).
- `price` (String) Prices define the unit cost, currency, and (optional) billing cycle for both recurring and one-time purchases of products.
[Products](https://api.stripe.com#products) help you track inventory or provisioning, and prices help you track payment terms. Different physical goods or levels of service should be represented by products, and pricing options should be represented by prices. This approach lets you change prices without having to change your provisioning scheme.

For example, you might have a single "gold" product that has prices for $10/month, $100/year, and €9 once.

Related guides: [Set up a subscription](https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/set-up-subscription), [create an invoice](https://docs.stripe.com/billing/invoices/create), and more about [products and prices](https://docs.stripe.com/products-prices/overview).
- `price_data` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Data used to generate a new [Price](https://docs.stripe.com/api/prices) object inline. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--price_data))
- `proration_behavior` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Determines how to handle [prorations](https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/prorations) when the billing cycle changes (e.g., when switching plans, resetting `billing_cycle_anchor=now`, or starting a trial), or if an item's `quantity` changes. The default value is `create_prorations`.
- `proration_date` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) If set, the proration will be calculated as though the subscription was updated at the given time. This can be used to apply the same proration that was previewed with the [upcoming invoice](/api/invoices/create_preview) endpoint.
- `quantity` (Number) The [quantity](https://docs.stripe.com/subscriptions/quantities) of the plan to which the customer should be subscribed.
- `tax_rates` (List of String) The tax rates which apply to this `subscription_item`. When set, the `default_tax_rates` on the subscription do not apply to this `subscription_item`.

### Read-Only

- `billed_until` (Number) The time period the subscription item has been billed for.
- `created` (Number) Time at which the object was created. Measured in seconds since the Unix epoch.
- `current_period_end` (Number) The end time of this subscription item's current billing period.
- `current_period_start` (Number) The start time of this subscription item's current billing period.
- `id` (String) Unique identifier for the object.
- `object` (String) String representing the object's type. Objects of the same type share the same value.

<a id="nestedatt--billing_thresholds"></a>
### Nested Schema for `billing_thresholds`

Required:

- `usage_gte` (Number) Usage threshold that triggers the subscription to create an invoice


<a id="nestedatt--discounts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `discounts`

Optional:

- `coupon` (String) ID of the coupon to create a new discount for.
- `discount` (String) ID of an existing discount on the object (or one of its ancestors) to reuse.
- `promotion_code` (String) ID of the promotion code to create a new discount for.


<a id="nestedatt--price_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `price_data`

Required:

- `currency` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).
- `product` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The ID of the [Product](https://docs.stripe.com/api/products) that this [Price](https://docs.stripe.com/api/prices) will belong to.
- `recurring` (Attributes, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The recurring components of a price such as `interval` and `interval_count`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--price_data--recurring))

Optional:

- `tax_behavior` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Only required if a [default tax behavior](https://docs.stripe.com/tax/products-prices-tax-categories-tax-behavior#setting-a-default-tax-behavior-(recommended)) was not provided in the Stripe Tax settings. Specifies whether the price is considered inclusive of taxes or exclusive of taxes. One of `inclusive`, `exclusive`, or `unspecified`. Once specified as either `inclusive` or `exclusive`, it cannot be changed.
- `unit_amount` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) A positive integer in cents (or local equivalent) (or 0 for a free price) representing how much to charge.
- `unit_amount_decimal` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Same as `unit_amount`, but accepts a decimal value in cents (or local equivalent) with at most 12 decimal places. Only one of `unit_amount` and `unit_amount_decimal` can be set.

<a id="nestedatt--price_data--recurring"></a>
### Nested Schema for `price_data.recurring`

Required:

- `interval` (String, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Specifies billing frequency. Either `day`, `week`, `month` or `year`.

Optional:

- `interval_count` (Number, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The number of intervals between subscription billings. For example, `interval=month` and `interval_count=3` bills every 3 months. Maximum of three years interval allowed (3 years, 36 months, or 156 weeks).
